title: Objects, Scope, Linkage, Storage Duration, and Lifetime · Object-Lifetime Lens: Address Reuse Generation
summary: An embedded controller caches addresses across callbacks while automatic, static, thread, and allocated objects begin and end at different times. Planned artifact: Add and verify the cmem address reuse generation increment in object lifetime and address ledger. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Treat a reused address as a new lifetime and preserve generation or identity evidence where stale handles are possible.
Identify precisely when each object lifetime begins and ends, including allocated storage and subobjects.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Predict Address Reuse Generation · Theory Address Reuse Generation
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory native-systems team handles memory case 1391. Produce bounded C evidence to validate object lifetime and generation evidence; change the size, lifetime, failure, or ownership condition; print the observed result and invariant. Instant code stays inside the PicoC practice subset. C23 syntax, current GCC and Clang, optimization, ABI, threads, sanitizers, analyzers, fuzzers, operating-system resources, foreign runtimes, load, and production behavior require named controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Treat a reused address as a new lifetime and preserve generation or identity evidence where stale handles are possible.
